3014	% NOTE: This affine lemma is closed directly on global sections, via the two named
3015	% obligations: lem:base_change_map_affine_local (locality reduction on the affine
3016	% opens of S') and lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange (the section-

3022	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:base_change_map_affine_local, lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}
3023	  The proof is the composite of the two named obligations, taken \emph{directly on
3024	  global sections}.
3025	
3026	  \medskip
3027	  \emph{First reduction (locality on \(S'\)).} By
3028	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} it suffices to prove that the
3029	  base-change map of Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an
3030	  isomorphism on sections over every affine open of \(S'\). This is exactly the
3031	  locality reduction Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}: restricting the
3032	  cartesian square to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R') \subseteq S'\) and
3033	  a chosen affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing \(g(U)\), the
3034	  hypothesis \([\mathrm{IsAffineHom}\ f]\) makes \(X\) restrict to
3035	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) makes it restrict
3036	  to \(\widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\). (It is here that quasi-coherence is
3037	  used: it is what licenses writing \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\).) Computing the
3038	  fibre product gives \(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\) and
3039	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) the tilde of the \((R' \otimes_R A)\)-module
3040	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\). The matter is thus reduced to the affine--affine
3041	  model, where the base-change map is a morphism \(\alpha\) of quasi-coherent
3042	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R')\)-modules.
3043	
3044	  \medskip
3045	  \emph{Passage to global sections.} The functor
3046	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \operatorname{ModuleCat}(R') \to
3047	  (\operatorname{Spec} R').\mathrm{Modules}\) is fully faithful with essential image
3048	  the quasi-coherent modules; equivalently the counit
3049	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{N})} \to \mathcal{N}\) is an isomorphism exactly when
3050	  \(\mathcal{N}\) is quasi-coherent. Source and target of \(\alpha\) are
3051	  quasi-coherent, so both counits are isomorphisms and, by their naturality,
3052	  \[
3053	    \mathrm{IsIso}\,\alpha
3054	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
3055	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl(\Gamma(\alpha)\bigr),
3056	  \]
3057	  where \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is a concrete \(R'\)-linear map in
3058	  \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\). The whole computation therefore stays at the level
3059	  of modules.
3060	
3061	  \medskip
3062	  \emph{Section-level identification (the second obligation).} By the section-level
3063	  computation Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}, the map
3064	  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\), with its domain \(\Gamma(g^*(f_*\widetilde{M}))\) identified with
3065	  \(R' \otimes_R M\) and its codomain \(\Gamma(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde{M})\) identified
3066	  with \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) through the two dictionaries
3067	  Lemmas~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} and~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso},
3068	  \emph{is} the canonical cancellation isomorphism
3069	  \[
3070	    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} :
3071	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M,
3072	    \qquad (r' \otimes a) \otimes m \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m)
3073	  \]
3074	  of \(R'\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}). This is the entire
3075	  content of the affine close, and it is performed \emph{directly on the module of
3076	  global sections} — there is no separate abstract adjoint-mate identification at the
3077	  level of sheaves. Because \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism with
3078	  no flatness hypothesis whatsoever, \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is an isomorphism, hence so is
3079	  \(\alpha\) on the affine chart, and by the locality reduction so is the global
3080	  base-change map. Flatness of \(g\) plays no role in the affine lemma; it enters only
3081	  in the global statement Theorem~\ref{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}, where it is
3082	  used to commute \(- \otimes_A B\) with the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\).
3083	\end{proof}

3141	[Finite affine cover with quasi-compact overlaps]
3142	  \label{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}
3143	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact}
3144	  A quasi-compact scheme \(X\) admits a \emph{finite} affine open cover
3145	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \dots \cup U_t\). If moreover \(X\) is
3146	  quasi-separated, then each pairwise intersection \(U_{ij} = U_i \cap U_j\) is
3147	  quasi-compact; and if \(X\) is separated, each \(U_{ij}\) is in fact affine.
3148	  \begin{proof}
3149	    The affine opens form a basis, so \(X\) is covered by affine opens; quasi-compactness
3150	    extracts a finite subcover \(U_1, \dots, U_t\) (by Mathlib's affine cover and the
3151	    finite-subcover property of a quasi-compact space). Quasi-separatedness says the
3152	    intersection of two quasi-compact opens is quasi-compact, so each \(U_{ij}\) is
3153	    quasi-compact. When \(X\) is separated the diagonal is a closed immersion, whence
3154	    the intersection of two affine opens is affine (Mathlib, for separated schemes).
3155	  \end{proof}

741	  pushforward dictionary as a natural transformation rather than merely a family of
742	  object isomorphisms. This is the input to the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints argument
743	  that produces the pullback companion Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}.
744	\end{lemma}
745	\begin{proof}
746	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
747	  The components are the object isomorphisms of Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso};
748	  naturality is the corresponding naturality square, which holds because every
749	  constituent of the comparison is identity-on-elements.
750	\end{proof}
751	
752	\begin{lemma}
753	
754	\leanok
755	[Affine pullback of a tilde-module]
756	  \label{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}
757	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_spec_tilde_iso}
758	  \uses{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:gammaPushforwardNatIso}
759	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, Lemma `lemma-widetilde-pullback', Tag 01I9
760	  % (\S\,Quasi-coherent sheaves on affines) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex,
761	  % L1241--1256).
762	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let
763	  % $(X, \mathcal{O}_X) = (\Spec(S), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(S)})$,
764	  % $(Y, \mathcal{O}_Y) = (\Spec(R), \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)})$
765	  % be affine schemes.
766	  % Let $\psi : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be a
767	  % morphism of affine schemes, corresponding to the ring map
768	  % $\psi^\sharp : R \to S$ (see Lemma \ref{lemma-category-affine-schemes}).
769	  % \begin{enumerate}
770	  % \item We have $\psi^* \widetilde M = \widetilde{S \otimes_R M}$
771	  % functorially in the $R$-module $M$.
772	  % \item We have $\psi_* \widetilde N = \widetilde{N_R}$ functorially
773	  % in the $S$-module $N$.
774	  % \end{enumerate}"
775	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, Lemma ``\(\widetilde M\) and pullback''
776	  (Tag 01I9), part (1).}
777	  Let \(\varphi : R \to R'\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and let \(M\)
778	  be an \(R\)-module. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of
779	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R')\)-modules
780	  \[
781	    \bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)^{*}\,\widetilde{M}
782	    \;\cong\;
783	    \widetilde{\bigl(R' \otimes_R M\bigr)},
784	  \]
785	  where \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi : \operatorname{Spec}(R') \to
786	  \operatorname{Spec}(R)\) is the induced morphism of affine schemes,
787	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) denotes the quasi-coherent sheaf associated to a module, and
788	  \(R' \otimes_R M\) is the base change of \(M\) along \(\varphi\) — the
789	  \(R'\)-module obtained by extension of scalars. The isomorphism is natural in
790	  \(M\). That is, pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\) back along
791	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, up to this canonical identification, the tilde
792	  of the base change of \(M\). It is the pullback companion of the affine
793	  pushforward dictionary Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}, and is part~(1)
794	  of the same Stacks lemma of which the pushforward formula is part~(2).
795	\end{lemma}
796	
797	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The first assertion follows from the identification in
798	% Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions}
799	% and the result of Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-restrict-quasi-coherent}."
800	\begin{proof}
801	  \uses{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
802	  Pullback along \(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\) is, on quasi-coherent sheaves, the
803	  extension-of-scalars operation \(- \otimes_R R'\) on modules transported through
804	  the \(\widetilde{(-)}\) / \(\operatorname{moduleSpec}\Gamma\) equivalence between
805	  modules and quasi-coherent sheaves. Concretely, the global sections of
806	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)^{*}\widetilde{M}\) are the base change
807	  \(R' \otimes_R M\): the pullback functor \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)^{*}\) is
808	  left adjoint to the pushforward \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\), and under
809	  the global-sections identification the latter is restriction of scalars along
810	  \(\varphi\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}); the left adjoint of
811	  restriction of scalars is extension of scalars \(M \mapsto R' \otimes_R M\). The
812	  comparison map is the unit of the base-change adjunction, and the universal
813	  property of base change identifies it with the canonical isomorphism
814	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{Spec}\varphi\bigr)^{*}\widetilde{M} \cong
815	  \widetilde{(R' \otimes_R M)}\). Naturality in \(M\) is the naturality of that
816	  unit. (One may equally read this off the standard-open description: on
817	  \(D(\varphi a)\) the localization \((R' \otimes_R M)[1/a]\) is
818	  \(R'[1/\varphi a] \otimes_{R[1/a]} M[1/a]\), which is the base change of the
819	  sections of \(\widetilde M\) over \(D(a)\).)
820	\end{proof}
821	
822	\subsection{The affine base-change lemma and its remaining obligations}
823	
824	The affine base-change lemma below proceeds \emph{directly on global sections}: it
825	reduces, via the locality criterion
826	Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} and the two affine dictionaries
827	Lemmas~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso} and~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso},
828	to two obligations that are isolated here as named blocks. Both are extracted from
829	the proof of Stacks ``Affine base change'', and both are absent from the pinned
830	Mathlib. The first is the \emph{locality reduction}: restricting to the affine opens
831	of \(S'\) reduces the isomorphism claim to the affine--affine model
832	\(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\), \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\),
833	\(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\). The second is the
834	\emph{section-level identification}: in that affine--affine model, the global-sections
835	incarnation \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) of the base-change map, transported through the two
836	proved tilde dictionaries, is the canonical cancellation isomorphism
837	\(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) — an isomorphism with no flatness hypothesis. This
838	is the honest cut of the affine close: locality on \(S'\), then a pure computation on
839	the module of global sections. No separate abstract adjoint-mate identification is
840	performed at the level of sheaves; the mate is unwound \emph{on sections}, where it
841	becomes \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) directly.
842	
843	\begin{lemma}
844	
845	\leanok
846	[Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map]
847	  \label{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}
848	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_map_affine_local}
849	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
850	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
851	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
852	  % "local on $S$ and $S'$" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
853	  % L920--926).
854	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The statement is local on $S$ and $S'$. Hence we may
855	  % assume $X = \Spec(A)$, $S = \Spec(R)$,
856	  % $S' = \Spec(R')$ and $\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}$
857	  % for some $A$-module $M$."
858	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
859	  change'' (proof, local-on-\(S\)-and-\(S'\) step).}
860	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be an affine morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
861	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, with a base-change square as above. The formation of the
862	  base-change map (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}) is compatible
863	  with restriction to affine opens of \(S'\): for each affine open \(U \subseteq S'\), the
864	  restriction of the base-change map to sections over \(U\) agrees with the
865	  base-change map of the square obtained by restricting along \(U\). Consequently,
866	  by the affine-open locality criterion
867	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the assertion that the base-change
868	  map is an isomorphism over an arbitrary base-change square \((S, S', X, X')\)
869	  reduces to the affine--affine case \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\),
870	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\), \(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
871	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\).
872	
873	\end{lemma}
874	
875	\begin{proof}
876	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
877	  By the affine-open locality criterion
878	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the base-change map is an isomorphism
879	  of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules as soon as its restriction to the sections over each
880	  affine open \(U \subseteq S'\) is an isomorphism. The only content beyond the bare criterion is to identify that
881	  restriction with the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out over
882	  \(U\). We obtain the identification by unfolding the definition of the map and
883	  pushing the restriction functor through each of its building blocks. The
884	  compatibility this needs is \emph{not} a new coherence theorem: it is the naturality
885	  of the adjunction transpose, together with the standard fact that pushforward
886	  commutes with restriction to an open of the base. We spell this out in three steps.
887	
888	  \medskip
889	  \emph{Step 1: unfold the map.} By
890	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}, the base-change map is the image,
891	  under the \((g^*, g_*)\)-adjunction transpose — the natural bijection
892	  \(\operatorname{Hom}(g^*A, B) \cong \operatorname{Hom}(A, g_*B)\) — of the morphism
893	  \[
894	    f_*\mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\,f_*(\eta_{\mathcal{F}})\,}
895	    f_*(g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F} = g_*\,f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F},
896	  \]
897	  where \(\eta_{\mathcal{F}} : \mathcal{F} \to (g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the unit of
898	  the \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction and the identification
899	  \(f_*(g')_* = g_*\,f'_*\) is the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward applied to the
900	  square's commutativity \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\). Thus the map is, by
901	  construction, the transpose of the \(f_*\)-image of a unit — a composite of
902	  adjunction data and pushforward functors, with no ingredient beyond these.
903	
904	  \medskip
905	  \emph{Step 2: restriction to \(U\) commutes with each building block.} Fix an affine
906	  open \(U \subseteq S'\) and write \(\rho_U\) for restriction to \(U\) —
907	  equivalently, the section functor \(\Gamma(U, -)\) on \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules,
908	  or pullback along the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow S'\). Restriction along
909	  \(U\) is a functor, and every ingredient of Step 1 is a natural transformation or a
910	  natural bijection; so applying \(\rho_U\) to the transpose-of-a-unit of Step 1
911	  returns the transpose-of-the-unit built from the restricted data. Concretely:
912	  \begin{itemize}
913	    \item[(a)] the unit \(\eta_{\mathcal{F}}\) of the \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction
914	      restricts to the unit of the corresponding adjunction on the restricted square,
915	      because units are natural in their object and pulling the cartesian square back
916	      along \(U \hookrightarrow S'\) yields again a cartesian square (with total space
917	      the open \((f')^{-1}(U)\) of \(X'\));
918	    \item[(b)] \(f_*\) of a restricted morphism is the restriction of \(f_*\) of the
919	      morphism, because pushforward commutes with restriction to an open of the base:
920	      for an open \(U\) of the base one has
921	      \((f_*\mathcal{G})|_U = (f|_{f^{-1}U})_*\bigl(\mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}U}\bigr)\),
922	      naturally in \(\mathcal{G}\) — and likewise for \((g')_*\) and \(f'_*\);
923	    \item[(c)] the \((g^*, g_*)\)-adjunction transpose is a bijection natural in both
924	      variables, so it commutes with restricting its source and target along \(U\).
925	  \end{itemize}
926	  Chaining (a), (b), (c) — i.e.\ transporting the transpose-of-unit composite of
927	  Step 1 through the restriction functor \(\rho_U\) and replacing each block by its
928	  restricted counterpart — shows that the restriction of the base-change map to
929	  sections over \(U\) is itself the base-change map formed from the cartesian square
930	  restricted over \(U\). Choosing an affine open
931	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing the image \(g(U)\), affineness of
932	  \(f\) (local on the base and stable under base change) makes the total space
933	  restrict to an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\), and quasi-coherence of
934	  \(\mathcal{F}\) makes it restrict to a tilde-module \(\widetilde{M}\) there; so this
935	  restricted map is exactly the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out
936	  over \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\) and \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). The whole
937	  identification is thus definitional plus the naturalities (a)--(c); it introduces no
938	  obligation beyond them.
939	
940	  \medskip
941	  \emph{Step 3: conclude the reduction.} By Step 2 the per-affine-open hypothesis
942	  required by the locality criterion Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} —
943	  that the base-change map restricts to an isomorphism on sections over each affine
944	  open \(U \subseteq S'\) — is, under the identification of Step 2, precisely the
945	  affine--affine section assertion. That assertion is supplied, with \emph{no} flatness
946	  hypothesis, by the section-level computation
947	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}, which identifies the
948	  affine--affine base-change map on global sections with the cancellation isomorphism
949	  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\). Applying the locality criterion
950	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} to this family of per-open
951	  isomorphisms shows that the base-change map is an isomorphism, as asserted.
952	\end{proof}
953	
954	\subsection{The section-level base-change identity}
955	
956	The section-level identification
957	Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange} below is the single
958	highest-leverage obligation of the affine close, and we prove it \emph{directly on
959	global sections}, with no abstract adjoint-mate tower. In the affine--affine model the
960	base-change map \(\theta\) is, after its domain and codomain are read through the two
961	proved tilde dictionaries, exactly the \(R'\)-base change of the algebraic unit of
962	extension of scalars; post-composing with the canonical regrouping isomorphism
963	\((A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \cong R' \otimes_R M\) identifies it with the canonical
964	base-change comparison, an isomorphism carrying no flatness hypothesis. The computation
965	factors through three named reads — the domain read, the codomain read, and the single
966	\emph{section-level identity} that the conjugated map is the base change of the unit —
967	together with the one Mathlib-absent crux identifying the pullback cone legs with the
968	\(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps of the tensor inclusions
969	(Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}). Two Mathlib results are recorded
970	first as dependency anchors.
971	
972	\begin{lemma}[Pullback of a pair of affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps]
973	  \label{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}
974	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackSpecIso}

6	import Mathlib
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FlatBaseChange
8	
9	/-!
10	# Flat base change for the pushforward, global (`H⁰`-as-equalizer) chain
11	
12	This file builds the global ("FBC-B") leg of the `i = 0` flat-base-change package:
13	`H⁰(X, F) = Γ(X, F)` of a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme is the equalizer of
14	a *finite* affine cover, and flat base change commutes with that finite equalizer.
15	
16	It is the companion of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FlatBaseChange`, which it imports
17	read-only (using the affine global-sections comparison as a per-term black box).
18	
19	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex` (FBC-B section).
20	-/
21	
22	universe u
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — finite affine covers with quasi-compact overlaps -/
29	
30	/-- A quasi-compact scheme admits a *finite* affine open cover; when it is moreover
31	quasi-separated, every pairwise intersection of cover members is quasi-compact.
32	
33	Project-local: packages `isCompact_iff_finite_and_eq_biUnion_affineOpens` (finite affine
34	subcover of `⊤`) with `quasiSeparatedSpace_iff_forall_affineOpens` (quasi-compact overlaps)
35	into the single combinatorial input feeding the finite sheaf-condition equalizer of the
36	`H⁰` flat-base-change argument. -/
37	theorem Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact (X : Scheme.{u})
38	    [CompactSpace X] [QuasiSeparatedSpace X] :
39	    ∃ s : Set X.affineOpens, s.Finite ∧ (⨆ i ∈ s, (i : X.Opens)) = ⊤ ∧
40	      ∀ U ∈ s, ∀ V ∈ s, IsCompact ((U : Set X) ∩ (V : Set X)) := by
41	  obtain ⟨s, hs, he⟩ :=
42	    (isCompact_iff_finite_and_eq_biUnion_affineOpens (U := (⊤ : X.Opens))).mp
43	      (by simpa using isCompact_univ (X := ↥X))
44	  refine ⟨s, hs, he.symm, ?_⟩
45	  intro U _ V _
46	  exact quasiSeparatedSpace_iff_forall_affineOpens.mp ‹_› U V
47	
48	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the global sections as a sheaf-condition equalizer -/
49	
50	open TopCat.Presheaf SheafConditionEqualizerProducts in
51	/-- For `M : X.Modules` and any open cover `U : ι → X.Opens`, the sheaf-condition fork
52	of the underlying abelian-group presheaf of `M` is a limit (an equalizer of products):
53	```
54	Γ(M, ⨆ i, U i) ⟶ ∏ i, Γ(M, U i) ⇉ ∏ (i,j), Γ(M, U i ⊓ U j).
55	```
56	This is the equalizer-products form of the sheaf condition specialised to the abelian
57	presheaf `M.presheaf` of a sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules. Combined with the finite affine cover
58	of `Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact` it computes `Γ(X, M) = Γ(M, ⊤)`
59	as a *finite* equalizer; that finiteness is what the flat-base-change argument needs to
60	commute `- ⊗_A B` past the equalizer.
61	
62	Project-local: packages `M.isSheaf` through Mathlib's
63	`isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts` at the level of `X.Modules`. -/
64	noncomputable def Modules.gammaIsLimitSheafConditionFork {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules)
65	    {ι : Type u} (U : ι → X.Opens) :
66	    IsLimit (fork M.presheaf U) :=
67	  ((isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts M.presheaf).mp M.isSheaf U).some
68	
69	open TopCat.Presheaf SheafConditionEqualizerProducts in
70	/-- Consolidation of the two preceding supplements: for a quasi-compact, quasi-separated
71	scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, there is a *finite* affine open cover `U : ι → X.Opens`
72	(`ι` finite, each `U i` affine, `⨆ i, U i = ⊤`, all pairwise intersections quasi-compact)
73	for which `Γ(X, M) = Γ(M, ⊤)` is the equalizer of the finite sheaf-condition fork of `U`.
74	
75	Project-local: combines `Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact` (L1) with
76	`Modules.gammaIsLimitSheafConditionFork` (L2) into the directly-usable "global sections as a
77	finite equalizer" input of the flat-base-change argument. -/
78	theorem Modules.exists_finite_affineCover_isLimit_sheafConditionFork (X : Scheme.{u})
79	    [CompactSpace X] [QuasiSeparatedSpace X] (M : X.Modules) :
80	    ∃ (ι : Type u) (_ : Finite ι) (U : ι → X.Opens),
81	      (∀ i, IsAffineOpen (U i)) ∧ (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
82	      (∀ i j, IsCompact ((U i : Set X) ∩ (U j : Set X))) ∧
83	      Nonempty (IsLimit (fork M.presheaf U)) := by
84	  obtain ⟨s, hs, hcov, hqc⟩ := X.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact
85	  have hfin : Finite ↥s := hs.to_subtype
86	  refine ⟨↥s, hfin, fun i => ((i : X.affineOpens) : X.Opens), fun i => i.1.2, ?_, ?_,
87	    ⟨Modules.gammaIsLimitSheafConditionFork M _⟩⟩
88	  · rw [← hcov, iSup_subtype']
89	  · rintro ⟨i, hi⟩ ⟨j, hj⟩
90	    exact hqc i hi j hj
91	
92	end AlgebraicGeometry

variable {F₀₁ : C₀ ⥤ C₁} {F₁₂ : C₁ ⥤ C₂} {F₀₂ : C₀ ⥤ C₂}
  {G₁₀ : C₁ ⥤ C₀} {G₂₁ : C₂ ⥤ C₁} {G₂₀ : C₂ ⥤ C₀}
  (adj₀₁ : F₀₁ ⊣ G₁₀) (adj₁₂ : F₁₂ ⊣ G₂₁) (adj₀₂ : F₀₂ ⊣ G₂₀)

/-- A natural transformation `G₂₀ ⟶ G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀` involving right adjoint functors
induces a natural transformation `F₀₁ ⋙ F₁₂ ⟶ F₀₂` between the corresponding
left adjoint functors. -/
@[simps! -isSimp]
def leftAdjointCompNatTrans (τ₀₁₂ : G₂₀ ⟶ G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀) :
    F₀₁ ⋙ F₁₂ ⟶ F₀₂ :=
  (conjugateEquiv adj₀₂ (adj₀₁.comp adj₁₂)).symm τ₀₁₂

/-- A natural isomorphism `G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀` involving right adjoint functors
induces a natural isomorphism `F₀₁ ⋙ F₁₂ ≅ F₀₂` between the corresponding
left adjoint functors. -/
@[simps! -isSimp]
def leftAdjointCompIso (e₀₁₂ : G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀) :
    F₀₁ ⋙ F₁₂ ≅ F₀₂ :=
  (conjugateIsoEquiv adj₀₂ (adj₀₁.comp adj₁₂)).symm e₀₁₂.symm

lemma leftAdjointCompIso_hom (e₀₁₂ : G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀) :
    (leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e₀₁₂).hom =
      leftAdjointCompNatTrans adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e₀₁₂.inv :=
  rfl

@[simp]
lemma conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv (e₀₁₂ : G₂₁ ⋙ G₁₀ ≅ G₂₀) :

      (leftAdjointCompIso adj₀₁ adj₁₂ adj₀₂ e₀₁₂).inv = e₀₁₂.hom := by
  dsimp only [leftAdjointCompIso]
  simp

end

% --- Mathlib dependency anchors for the conjugate-side re-encoding of the leg-reindex (Open Q2) ---
% These five categorical results are the tools of the conjugate-side (mate / Beck--Chevalley)
% proof route that replaces the abandoned direct-on-sections telescoping; all are supplied verbatim
% by Mathlib (CategoryTheory/Adjunction/Mates.lean and .../CompositionIso.lean).

\begin{lemma}[Conjugation is an equivalence on isomorphisms]
  \label{lem:conjugateIsoEquiv_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.conjugateIsoEquiv}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : L_1 \dashv R_1\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : L_2 \dashv R_2\) be two adjunctions
  between the same pair of categories. The conjugation (mate) bijection on natural transformations
  restricts to an equivalence on natural \emph{isomorphisms},
  \[
    \operatorname{conjIso}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_2) :
      (L_2 \cong L_1) \;\simeq\; (R_1 \cong R_2),
  \]
  carrying an isomorphism of left adjoints to the conjugate isomorphism of right adjoints, and back. In
  particular the conjugate of an isomorphism is an isomorphism: this is the certificate that makes the
  affine flat-base-change comparison invertible without inspecting its concrete sheaf assembly.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[The iterated mate of a left-adjoint square is the conjugate (Beck--Chevalley)]
  \label{lem:iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For a square of functors whose four boundary functors are all left adjoints, with adjunctions
  \(\mathrm{adj}_1, \dots, \mathrm{adj}_4\), the iterated mate (the Beck--Chevalley \(2\)-cell of the
  pasted square) of a \(2\)-cell \(\alpha\) equals the conjugate of \(\alpha\) for the two composite
  adjunctions:
  \[
    \operatorname{mate}\bigl(\operatorname{mate}(\alpha)\bigr) \;=\;
      \operatorname{conj}\bigl(\mathrm{adj}_1.\mathrm{adj}_4,\ \mathrm{adj}_3.\mathrm{adj}_2\bigr)\,\alpha .
  \]
  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateIsoEquiv_mathlib} this is the abstract reason a Beck--Chevalley
  transformation is a natural isomorphism exactly when \(\alpha\) is — the conceptual home of the
  iso-ness of the affine comparison \(g^* f_*\widetilde M \to f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\), which the
  project realises directly as a conjugate of the pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Composite-adjunction comparison from a right-adjoint isomorphism]
  \label{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_{01} : F_{01} \dashv G_{10}\), \(\mathrm{adj}_{12} : F_{12} \dashv G_{21}\) and
  \(\mathrm{adj}_{02} : F_{02} \dashv G_{20}\) be adjunctions, and let
  \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\) be a natural isomorphism comparing the composite right adjoint
  with \(G_{20}\). Then there is a canonical natural isomorphism of the corresponding left adjoints
  \[
    \operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(e) : F_{01} \circ F_{12} \;\cong\; F_{02},
  \]
  namely the conjugate of \(e^{-1}\). It builds the comparison for a composite of two adjunctions out of
  a single right-adjoint comparison, carrying no propositional equality data.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[The conjugate of the composite-comparison inverse is the right-adjoint isomorphism]
  \label{lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}, the conjugation bijection between the
  composite adjunction \(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{adj}_{12}\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_{02}\) sends the inverse
  of the composite comparison \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(e)\) back to the hom of the
  right-adjoint isomorphism \(e\):
  \[
    \operatorname{conj}\bigl(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{adj}_{12},\ \mathrm{adj}_{02}\bigr)
      \bigl(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(e)^{-1}\bigr) \;=\; e^{\mathrm{hom}} .
  \]
  This is the one-line bridge that turns a coherence among composite-adjunction comparisons into an
  equation on the right-adjoint side, where the conjugate simp set closes it.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Transposed unit-across-conjugate coherence]
  \label{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_symm_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv_symm}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : L_1 \dashv R_1\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : L_2 \dashv R_2\) be adjunctions with
  units \(\eta_1,\eta_2\), and let \(\alpha : R_1 \Rightarrow R_2\) be a natural transformation of the
  right adjoints. Then for every object \(c\),
  \[
    (\eta_1)_c \,\circ\, \alpha_{L_1 c}
      \;=\; (\eta_2)_c \,\circ\, R_2\bigl(\bigl(\operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_2)^{-1}\alpha\bigr)_c\bigr),
  \]
  the transposed (inverse-conjugation) form of the unit-across-conjugate coherence
  Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}. This is the Seam~1 tool used one functor layer up in the
  conjugate-side leg-reindex: the cross-layer naturality of the pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison enters
  through this identity, not through a positional naturality rewrite.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[The iterated mate of a left-adjoint square is the conjugate (Beck--Chevalley)]
  \label{lem:iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.iterated_mateEquiv_conjugateEquiv}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For a square of functors whose four boundary functors are all left adjoints, with adjunctions
  \(\mathrm{adj}_1, \dots, \mathrm{adj}_4\), the iterated mate (the Beck--Chevalley \(2\)-cell of the
  pasted square) of a \(2\)-cell \(\alpha\) equals the conjugate of \(\alpha\) for the two composite
  adjunctions:
  \[
    \operatorname{mate}\bigl(\operatorname{mate}(\alpha)\bigr) \;=\;
      \operatorname{conj}\bigl(\mathrm{adj}_1.\mathrm{adj}_4,\ \mathrm{adj}_3.\mathrm{adj}_2\bigr)\,\alpha .
  \]
  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateIsoEquiv_mathlib} this is the abstract reason a Beck--Chevalley
  transformation is a natural isomorphism exactly when \(\alpha\) is — the conceptual home of the
  iso-ness of the affine comparison \(g^* f_*\widetilde M \to f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\), which the
  project realises directly as a conjugate of the pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Composite-adjunction comparison from a right-adjoint isomorphism]
  \label{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_{01} : F_{01} \dashv G_{10}\), \(\mathrm{adj}_{12} : F_{12} \dashv G_{21}\) and
  \(\mathrm{adj}_{02} : F_{02} \dashv G_{20}\) be adjunctions, and let
  \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\) be a natural isomorphism comparing the composite right adjoint
  with \(G_{20}\). Then there is a canonical natural isomorphism of the corresponding left adjoints
  \[
    \operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(e) : F_{01} \circ F_{12} \;\cong\; F_{02},
  \]
  namely the conjugate of \(e^{-1}\). It builds the comparison for a composite of two adjunctions out of
  a single right-adjoint comparison, carrying no propositional equality data.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[The conjugate of the composite-comparison inverse is the right-adjoint isomorphism]
  \label{lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}, the conjugation bijection between the
  composite adjunction \(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{adj}_{12}\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_{02}\) sends the inverse
  of the composite comparison \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(e)\) back to the hom of the
  right-adjoint isomorphism \(e\):
  \[
    \operatorname{conj}\bigl(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{adj}_{12},\ \mathrm{adj}_{02}\bigr)
      \bigl(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}(e)^{-1}\bigr) \;=\; e^{\mathrm{hom}} .
  \]
  This is the one-line bridge that turns a coherence among composite-adjunction comparisons into an
  equation on the right-adjoint side, where the conjugate simp set closes it.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Transposed unit-across-conjugate coherence]
  \label{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_symm_mathlib}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv_symm}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : L_1 \dashv R_1\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : L_2 \dashv R_2\) be adjunctions with
  units \(\eta_1,\eta_2\), and let \(\alpha : R_1 \Rightarrow R_2\) be a natural transformation of the
  right adjoints. Then for every object \(c\),
  \[
    (\eta_1)_c \,\circ\, \alpha_{L_1 c}
      \;=\; (\eta_2)_c \,\circ\, R_2\bigl(\bigl(\operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1,\mathrm{adj}_2)^{-1}\alpha\bigr)_c\bigr),
  \]
  the transposed (inverse-conjugation) form of the unit-across-conjugate coherence
  Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}. This is the Seam~1 tool used one functor layer up in the
  conjugate-side leg-reindex: the cross-layer naturality of the pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison enters
  through this identity, not through a positional naturality rewrite.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Pseudofunctoriality of the pullback--pushforward unit]

[Codomain read with the cone legs as free variables]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor,
        lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso,
        lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
  It is the leg-parametrised form of the codomain read
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}. Fix \(\psi : R \to R'\),
  \(\varphi : R \to A\) and an \(A\)-module \(M\), let \(e : X' \xrightarrow{\sim}
  \operatorname{Spec}(A \otimes_R R')\) be the canonical isomorphism and
  \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) the tensor
  inclusions. For morphisms \(g' : X' \to \operatorname{Spec} A\) and
  \(f' : X' \to \operatorname{Spec} R'\) together with leg-equality proofs witnessing
  \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\),
  there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R'\)-modules
  \[
    \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\flat} :
    \Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)
    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
    \operatorname{restr}_{\iota_{R'}}\bigl(\operatorname{ext}_{\iota_A} M\bigr)
    \;=\; (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M .
  \]
  Here the construction is parametrised by the leg-equality proofs
  \((g', f', h_{\mathrm{fst}}, h_{\mathrm{snd}})\), which the isomorphism carries as data, with
  \(g'\) and \(f'\) genuine free variables in the abstract statement. Instantiating at the literal
  projections \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\) (with the cone-leg
  identifications of Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}) recovers
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}. Carrying the legs as free variables here is
  exactly what makes the downstream reindex work: with \(g'\) identified with
  \(e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) \emph{definitionally}, the unit expansion and eCancel
  telescoping apply uniformly to this parametrised read. The obstruction at the \emph{literal}
  projection legs — where \(\operatorname{pr}_1\) is merely propositionally equal to that composite
  and the read's type bakes the leg in — means a transport across the propositional leg-equality
  meets a dependent-motive obstruction, which is precisely why the reindex must pass to this
  leg-parametrised form rather than distributing inline.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor,
        lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso,
        lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
  The construction is verbatim that of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read},
  only with \(g', f'\) carried together with their leg-equality proofs rather than as the
  literal pullback projections. Using those proofs, which witness
  \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and
  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\), the pullback dictionary
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} along \(\iota_A\) reads \((g')^*\widetilde M\)
  as the extension of scalars \(\operatorname{ext}_{\iota_A} M\), the unit isomorphism of
  the \(e\)-pullback equivalence (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}) absorbs
  the isomorphism leg \(e\), and the pushforward dictionary
  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso} along \(\iota_{R'}\) reads \(f'_*\) of it as
  restriction of scalars back to \(R'\). The identification of the legs with the
  tensor-inclusion \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps is Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}.
  The literal-projection form of Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read} is the
  instantiation at \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\) carrying those
  proofs.
\end{proof}

[Codomain read assembled from the composite-adjunction comparison]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
        lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib,
        lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib}
  % NOTE: re-encoded (Open Q2). The codomain-read comparison object is now built ON THE CONJUGATE
  % SIDE, as the composite-adjunction comparison `leftAdjointCompIso` of the FREE morphisms `e.hom`
  % (the canonical square isomorphism `pullbackSpecIso`) and `Spec.map inclA` (the left tensor
  % inclusion), with the pasted square entering through `pushforwardCongr comm` / `pushforwardComp`.
  % It carries NO leg-equality proof data `hfst`/`hsnd`: the legs are the EXPLICIT composite
  % `e.hom ≫ Spec.map inclA`, so no propositional transport is baked into the object. This is the
  % re-cut that turns the downstream reindex coherence into a conjugate-component identity rather
  % than a positional rewrite under the X.Modules instance diamond.
  It is the composite-adjunction form of the codomain read
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}. Fix \(\psi : R \to R'\),
  \(\varphi : R \to A\) and an \(A\)-module \(M\); let \(e : X' \xrightarrow{\sim}
  \operatorname{Spec}(A \otimes_R R')\) be the canonical isomorphism and
  \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\) the tensor
  inclusions. Reading the codomain \(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\) along the \emph{explicit composite}
  legs \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\),
  there is a canonical isomorphism of \(R'\)-modules
  \[
    \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\flat} :
    \Gamma\bigl(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde M\bigr)
    \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }
    \operatorname{restr}_{\iota_{R'}}\bigl(\operatorname{ext}_{\iota_A} M\bigr)
    \;=\; (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M ,
  \]
  assembled as the composite-adjunction comparison \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}) of the two free legs \(e\) and
  \(\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) — the square's commutativity entering through the pushforward
  congruence \(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\) and the pushforward composition coherence
  \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}\) — followed by the affine pullback dictionary
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}) along \(\iota_A\) and the affine pushforward dictionary
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}) along \(\iota_{R'}\). Because the comparison object is
  built from the free morphisms with \emph{no} leg-equality proof carried, its conjugate inverts to
  the right-adjoint comparison in one step
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib}); this is what frees the downstream
  reindex coherence from any transport across a propositional leg-equality. Instantiating at the
  literal projection legs \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\) recovers
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
        lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}
  The construction is the assembly described in the statement, and it carries no equality-of-legs
  data, so there is nothing to transport. The composite-adjunction comparison
  \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}) packages the two
  free legs \(e\), \(\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) together with the pasted square's pushforward
  coherences (\(\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\), \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}\)) into a single
  right-adjoint-read isomorphism; composing it with the two affine dictionaries
  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}, \ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}) yields
  \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}^{\flat}\). Its agreement with
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read} at the literal projection legs is the cone-leg
  factorization.
\end{proof}

[The pushforward pseudofunctor reindex with the cone legs as free variables]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs,
        def:base_change_mate_inner_value,
        lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id, lem:gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_distributeCollapse,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_cancelEUnit,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_cancelPullbackComp,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_survivor}
  % NOTE (iter-031, declaration-ordering constraint for the formalizer): in the Lean file the
  % three eCancel atoms (base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_eUnit / _pushforwardComp / _pullbackComp)
  % are currently defined AFTER base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs, so they are OUT OF SCOPE at the
  % `_legs` sorry (confirmed: "Unknown identifier base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_eUnit" at the legs
  % sorry). To close `_legs` the next prover must EITHER move those three atoms (and
  % base_change_mate_unit_value) above `_legs`, OR inline the canceller content directly
  % (eUnit = IsIso of the pullbackPushforward unit via pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso;
  % pullbackComp = (pullbackComp _ _).hom_inv_id_app; pushforwardComp = the pre-`_legs`
  % gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id). The link sub-lemmas L3-L5 (cancelEUnit / cancelPullbackComp
  % / survivor) must then be written between the atoms and `_legs`. The keyed-rewriting family
  % (rw/simp/erw) is conclusively dead against the X.Modules instance diamond -- the residual
  % canceller splice is term-mode only (congrArg/Functor.congr_map/.trans + defeq-bridging exact).
  \emph{Why the legs must be parametrised.} The projection leg \(\operatorname{pr}_1\) is only
  propositionally equal to the composite \(e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}), not definitionally, and the codomain read is
  a type-indexed construction in the leg; transporting the unit expansion across this propositional
  leg-equality therefore fails. Carrying the legs as free parameters and specialising them to their
  composite form makes the identification available by reflexivity, which is exactly what enables
  the unit expansion and the ensuing telescoping.

  It is the leg-parametrised form of the whole Seam~2 reindex identity
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}. Fix \(\psi : R \to R'\),
  \(\varphi : R \to A\) and an \(A\)-module \(M\). For legs
  \(g' : X' \to \operatorname{Spec} A\) and \(f' : X' \to \operatorname{Spec} R'\) of the pullback
  square together with leg-equality proofs witnessing \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\),
  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) and the square's commutativity
  \(w : g' \circ \operatorname{Spec}\varphi = f' \circ \operatorname{Spec}\psi\) — with the
  codomain read supplied by Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs} — the inner
  composite \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) built from the \((g')\)-unit and the pushforward
  coherences, read on global sections over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\), equals the canonical
  \(R\)-linear map \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of
  Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}. Instantiating at the literal projection legs
  \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\) recovers the concrete
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs,
        def:base_change_mate_inner_value,
        lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id, lem:gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_distributeCollapse,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_cancelEUnit,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_cancelPullbackComp,
        lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_survivor}
  \emph{Step (i).} With the legs carried as parameters, substitute the cone-leg identifications
  \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\); the
  commutativity hypothesis \(w\), the adjunction index, and the codomain-read argument all transform
  uniformly under this (well-typed) substitution.

  \emph{Step (ii): \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections the
  inverse pushforward-composition factor collapses to the identity
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id}) and the congruence factor collapses to the
  identity (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom}), leaving the unit factor
  \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_*(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M})\) and the surviving
  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',\operatorname{Spec}\varphi})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) coherence for step~(iii).

  \emph{Step (iii): the mate-unwinding crux, as a chain of clean-term links.} After expanding the
  bare \((g')\)-unit (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_unitExpand}), the comparison
  composite is reduced to \(\rho\) by chaining the four clean-term links below. Each is an equality
  between two freshly elaborated terms, so the \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module instance diamond is absent in
  its own statement and it is provable by ordinary functoriality / isomorphism cancellation; the
  links chain by transitivity of equality:
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item \(L_{1}\) distributes the expanded unit through the single composite functor
      \((\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*} \circ \Gamma_R\) into its \(\Gamma\)-image factors and collapses
      the transparent \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}\) factor
      (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_distributeCollapse});
    \item \(L_2\) cancels the \(e\)-unit factor against the inverse \(e\)-unit baked into the codomain
      read (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_cancelEUnit});
    \item \(L_3\) cancels the trailing \(\mathrm{pullbackComp}\) factor against the inverse built into
      the codomain read
      (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_cancelPullbackComp});
    \item \(L_4\) evaluates the lone surviving affine unit as \(\rho\)
      (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs_link_survivor}).
  \end{enumerate}
  The transitive composite \(L_1 \cdot L_2 \cdot L_3 \cdot L_4\) is the desired identity. A
  single coherence identification then aligns the assembled composite with \(\rho\); this is the
  only point at which the ambient associativity ambiguity is crossed, which is exactly why the
  telescoping is cut into separately stated steps.
\end{proof}

[The pushforward pseudofunctor reindex at the explicit composite leg]
  \label{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_fstar_reindex_legs}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs, def:base_change_mate_inner_value,
        lem:conjugateIsoEquiv_mathlib, lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib,
        lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib,
        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib, lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_symm_mathlib,
        lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id, lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id,
        lem:gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value,
        lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
  % RECIPE (conjugate-side re-encoding, Open Q2). The coherence is proved as an identity of
  % `Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv` COMPONENTS, never as a positional rewrite under the X.Modules
  % instance diamond:
  %   1. Read the codomain comparison as `leftAdjointCompIso` of the free legs `e.hom`,
  %      `Spec.map inclA` (lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs); it carries no `hfst/hsnd`.
  %   2. Apply `(Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv …).injective`: it suffices to check the identity after
  %      conjugation.
  %   3. Replace each locked right-adjoint-side natural transformation by a fresh free variable via
  %      `(conjugateEquiv …).surjective` (`obtain ⟨τ, rfl⟩`), so no diamond-locked literal remains.
  %   4. Discharge with the reassoc conjugate simp set (`conjugateEquiv_comp`,
  %      `conjugateEquiv_symm_comp`, `conjugateEquiv_whiskerLeft`/`_whiskerRight`,
  %      `conjugateEquiv_associator_hom`), the project's `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` and
  %      `conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv`, and the pushforward-side Γ-collapses
  %      (`gammaMap_pushforwardComp_*`, `gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom`).
  %   5. The cross-layer naturality of `gammaPushforwardIso ψ` enters as `unit_conjugateEquiv_symm`
  %      composed ONE functor layer up via `conjugateEquiv_comp` — the Seam-1 tool, not a positional
  %      naturality rewrite.
  % The earlier direct-on-sections granular chain (`…_legs_unitExpand`/`…_gammaDistribute`/`…_link_*`
  % and the `inner_eCancel_*` family) is SUPERSEDED by this route (see their NOTEs).
  \emph{Why the legs are taken as the explicit composite.} The reindex coherence is read along the
  explicit composite leg \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) (and
  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\)), with \(e\) the canonical square isomorphism
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackSpecIso_mathlib}) and \(\iota_A,\iota_{R'}\) the tensor inclusions. Taking
  the legs as this composite — rather than as the literal projections, which are only propositionally
  equal to it — is what lets the codomain read be assembled from the free morphisms
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs}) and the coherence be discharged on the
  conjugate side, where the \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module instance diamond never bites.

  Fix \(\psi : R \to R'\), \(\varphi : R \to A\) and an \(A\)-module \(M\). The inner composite
  \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) built from the \((g')\)-unit and the pushforward coherences, read on global
  sections over \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) with the codomain supplied by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs}, equals the canonical \(R\)-linear map
  \(\rho : m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\) of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}.
  Instantiating at the literal projection legs \(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\),
  \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\) recovers the concrete Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs, def:base_change_mate_inner_value,
        lem:conjugateIsoEquiv_mathlib, lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib,
        lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib,
        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib, lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_symm_mathlib,
        lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id, lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id,
        lem:gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom, lem:base_change_mate_unit_value,
        lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
  The identity is proved entirely on the conjugate side of the mate calculus, never as a positional
  rewrite of the locked section composite.

  \emph{Conjugate reformulation.} The codomain comparison is assembled, by
  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs}, as the composite-adjunction comparison
  \(\operatorname{leftAdjointCompIso}\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:leftAdjointCompIso_mathlib}) of the two free
  legs \(e\) and \(\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), carrying no leg-equality data. Both sides of the
  desired identity are values of the conjugation bijection
  \(\operatorname{conj} = \operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_L,\mathrm{adj}_R)\)
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateIsoEquiv_mathlib}); since that bijection is injective, it suffices to prove
  the equality of their conjugate preimages on the right-adjoint side.

  \emph{Lifting the locked transformations to free variables.} Each natural transformation appearing
  on the right-adjoint side is, by surjectivity of the conjugation bijection, the conjugate of a free
  left-adjoint-side transformation; replacing each by such a free preimage removes every
  diamond-locked literal from the goal. The remaining identity is then a formal consequence of the
  coherence of the conjugation operation.

  \emph{Discharge by the conjugate coherences.} The conjugate of the inverse composite comparison is
  the right-adjoint isomorphism \(e\) in one step
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_inv_mathlib}); the conjugate of the inverse
  pullback coherence is the pushforward coherence
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}); the conjugation-composition,
  conjugation-whiskering and conjugation-associator coherences fuse the per-adjunction conjugates of
  \(\mathrm{adj}_L\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_R\) (the conjugate-side form of the paste-of-mates); and on the
  pushforward side the transparent composition and congruence coherences collapse on sections
  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id},
  \ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id}, \ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardCongr_hom}).

  \emph{Cross-layer naturality.} The one genuinely cross-layer step — the naturality of the
  pushforward \(\Gamma\)-comparison \(\gamma_\psi\) (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}) — enters as
  the transposed unit-across-conjugate coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_symm_mathlib},
  composed one functor layer up through the conjugation-composition coherence (the Seam~1 tool, raised
  one layer), rather than as a positional naturality rewrite. The lone surviving affine
  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit evaluates by Seam~1
  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}) to \(\rho\)
  (Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Global sections as a finite equalizer over a finite affine cover]
  \label{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.exists_finite_affineCover_isLimit_sheafConditionFork}
  \uses{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs, lem:gamma_finite_equalizer}
  Let \(X\) be a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme and \(M : X.\mathrm{Modules}\). Then there
  \emph{exists} a finite affine open cover \(\{U_i\}_{i \in \iota}\) of \(X\) — \(\iota\) finite, each
  \(U_i\) affine, \(\bigsqcup_i U_i = \top\), all pairwise intersections \(U_i \cap U_j\)
  quasi-compact — whose sheaf-condition fork of the underlying presheaf of \(M\) is a limit:
  \[
    \Gamma(X, M) \;=\; \operatorname{eq}\Bigl(
      \textstyle\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, M) \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, M)
    \Bigr).
  \]
  This consolidates the finite affine cover Lemma~\ref{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs} with the
  sheaf-condition equalizer Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer} into a single existential, the
  directly-usable ``global sections as a finite equalizer'' input of the flat-base-change argument.
  \begin{proof}
    \uses{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs, lem:gamma_finite_equalizer}
    Extract a finite affine cover with quasi-compact overlaps by
    Lemma~\ref{lem:finite_affine_cover_qcqs}, and apply the sheaf-condition equalizer
    Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer} (in its form valid for any open family) to that cover; the
    pair witnesses the existential.
  \end{proof}
\end{lemma}

\subsection{The \texorpdfstring{$A$}{A}-linear equalizer presentation (FBC-B build-ahead)}

The separated-case flat base change Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_base_change_separated} can be organised by
presenting \(\Gamma(X,\mathcal{F})\) as an honest \(\operatorname{LinearMap.eqLocus}\) over the global
ring \(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X)\) and invoking the Mathlib base-change-of-equalizer equivalence
\(\operatorname{tensorEqLocusEquiv}\) (recorded as the dependency anchor
Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}, which pins exactly
\texttt{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}). This sub-lane is independent of the affine section-level
computation; its three ingredients are recorded here as build-ahead blocks. The Lean pins below name
to-be-built declarations.

\begin{lemma}[$A$-module structure on cover sections by restriction of scalars]
  \label{lem:gamma_amodule_restriction}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.gammaCoverRestrictScalars}
  \uses{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}
  Let \(X\) be a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme, \(M : X.\mathrm{Modules}\), and write
  \(A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) for the ring of global sections of the structure sheaf. For a finite
  affine cover \(\{U_i\}\) with overlaps \(U_{ij}\), the structure-sheaf restriction ring maps
  \(A = \Gamma(X,\mathcal{O}_X) \to \Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{O}_X)\) and
  \(A \to \Gamma(U_{ij},\mathcal{O}_X)\) make each module of sections \(\Gamma(U_i, M)\),
  \(\Gamma(U_{ij}, M)\) an \(A\)-module by restriction of scalars; moreover the restriction maps of
  \(M\) along inclusions of opens are \(\Gamma(X,-)\)-semilinear over those restriction ring maps,
  hence \(A\)-linear after restriction of scalars.
  \begin{proof}
    \uses{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover}
    Each \(\Gamma(U_i,M)\) is a module over \(\Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{O}_X)\); restricting scalars along
    the ring map \(A \to \Gamma(U_i,\mathcal{O}_X)\) gives the \(A\)-module structure, and likewise on
    \(U_{ij}\). A presheaf restriction of \(M\) over an inclusion \(V \subseteq U\) is additive and
    semilinear over the corresponding restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\); since both the
    \(\mathcal{O}_X\)- and \(M\)-restrictions are compatible with the maps out of the global sections
    \(A\), the restriction becomes \(A\)-linear once scalars are restricted to \(A\).
  \end{proof}
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[$A$-linear product restriction maps of the cover]
  \label{lem:gamma_alinear_res_maps}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.gammaCoverResMapsALinear}
  \uses{lem:gamma_amodule_restriction, lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib}
  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_amodule_restriction}, the two parallel restriction maps of
  the sheaf-condition fork,
  \[
    \mathrm{leftRes},\ \mathrm{rightRes} :
      \prod_i \Gamma(U_i, M) \;\rightrightarrows\; \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, M),
  \]
  (the maps \(\mathrm{SheafConditionEqualizerProducts.leftRes}/\mathrm{rightRes}\)) are \(A\)-linear,
  i.e.\ read as morphisms in \(\operatorname{ModuleCat} A\) under the restriction-of-scalars
  \(A\)-module structures of Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_amodule_restriction}.
  \begin{proof}
    \uses{lem:gamma_amodule_restriction, lem:sheaf_equalizer_products_mathlib}
    Each component of \(\mathrm{leftRes}\) and \(\mathrm{rightRes}\) is a presheaf restriction of
    \(M\) along an inclusion \(U_{ij} \subseteq U_i\) (resp.\ \(U_{ij} \subseteq U_j\)), which is
    \(A\)-linear by Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_amodule_restriction}; a finite product of \(A\)-linear maps is
    \(A\)-linear.
  \end{proof}
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[$\Gamma(X,M)$ as an $A$-linear equalizer locus]
  \label{lem:gamma_eqLocus_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.gammaEqLocusIso}
  \uses{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover, lem:gamma_alinear_res_maps}
  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_alinear_res_maps}, there is an \(A\)-linear isomorphism
  \[
    \Gamma(X, M) \;\cong\; \operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{leftRes}, \mathrm{rightRes})
      \;\subseteq\; \prod_i \Gamma(U_i, M),
  \]
  identifying the global sections with the submodule on which the two product restriction maps agree.
  Combined with the base-change-of-equalizer equivalence
  Lemma~\ref{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}, this presents flat base change of
  \(\Gamma(X,M)\) as \(- \otimes_A B\) applied to the locus.
  \begin{proof}
    \uses{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover, lem:gamma_alinear_res_maps}
    By Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_finite_equalizer_cover} the global sections \(\Gamma(X,M)\) are the limit
    of the sheaf-condition fork in abelian groups; the limit of a parallel pair is the equalizer,
    whose underlying set is \(\{s : \mathrm{leftRes}\,s = \mathrm{rightRes}\,s\}\), i.e.\ the set-level
    locus \(\operatorname{eqLocus}(\mathrm{leftRes},\mathrm{rightRes})\). The forgetful functor
    \(\operatorname{forget}_2 : \operatorname{ModuleCat} A \to \mathbf{Ab}\) reflects this limit, so the
    identification upgrades to an \(A\)-linear isomorphism with the \(A\)-module
    \(\operatorname{eqLocus}\) of the \(A\)-linear maps of Lemma~\ref{lem:gamma_alinear_res_maps}.
  \end{proof}
\end{lemma}
